description: Single-source shortest-path cost modelled on the dijkstar library: findPathCost/edgeWeight/reachable with witness-minimality specs anchored on a frozen walk notion (isWalk/walkCost). The reference impl is Bellman-Ford relaxation; minimality is the load-bearing obligation. The achievability (witness), minimality, completeness, and walk-reachability obligations are stated in their strongest UNBOUNDED form — quantifying over walks of arbitrary length, so each subsumes the corresponding fuel-bounded statement (the bounded variants are not stated separately). Beyond the single-graph optimality laws (witness with a bounded simple-walk certificate, unbounded minimality, unbounded completeness, triangle, both-leg sub-path optimality, predecessor tightness, all-pairs subadditivity), the suite includes hidden-invariant obligations whose clean end-statements conceal a discovered strengthened induction hypothesis: append monotonicity of cost and reachability (cross-graph walk-lifting — appended edges never worsen an optimum), front-edge relaxation and tight-first-hop laws (the source-side mirror of the predecessor/triangle bounds, via a first-edge walk peel), and prefix-node reachability. Scope: the benchmark models shortest-path COST only (`find_path(...).total_cost`), NOT path reconstruction — there is no returned node/edge sequence. `edgeWeight` and `reachable` are derived observers, and edge weights are nonnegative `Nat`.
